rationales.self_direction: He rejects pressure from colleagues and says "I'm not going to apologize" for teaching the way he thinks matters.
rationales.conformity: He knowingly defies staff norms by continuing his criticized "field trip habit" instead of falling in line with test-prep expectations.
rationales.benevolence: He centers one student's engagement, saying it mattered to watch Marcus Jr. "actually engage with something for the first time all year."
rationales.universalism: He wants students to see "they're part of something bigger" and connect with food systems, earthworms, and the natural world.

rationales.tradition: He avoids participating in the family's "60th anniversary dinner" and admits his Cantonese is "terrible" because he never practices, distancing himself from a heritage obligation.
rationales.benevolence: He "let it go to voicemail" even though he knows his grandmother is reaching out and feels he is "letting her down".

rationales.self_direction: He chooses his own teaching approach despite the warning about "going off curriculum" and plans to "tell the real story" anyway.
rationales.conformity: After being told to be careful, he still plans to work around the official materials instead of simply following expectations.
rationales.tradition: He grounds the lesson in his great-grandfather's exclusion and plans to bring "my family photos" into class.
rationales.universalism: He objects to minimizing the Chinese Exclusion Act and wants students to hear a fuller account of historical injustice.

rationales.benevolence: He describes teaching as being for moments when kids discover something meaningful and says "that's what I want them to carry with them."
rationales.universalism: The whole reflection celebrates interconnection: "we must be connected to each other too and to the fish and to the trees" and "what we do matters beyond ourselves."

rationales.hedonism: He buys the strawberries because he was "tired" and "wanted something sweet," prioritizing immediate comfort.
rationales.universalism: He knowingly makes a choice he sees as unsustainable, dwelling on fruit "wrapped in plastic" and admitting his action "doesn't align" with what he teaches about sustainability.

rationales.tradition: He commits to giving the anniversary speech "in Cantonese" because the restaurant story belongs in "the language it's always been spoken in."
rationales.benevolence: He takes on the speech because "it's important to her" and to "the whole family, to the community really."
He pushed for the assembly because he remembers feeling alone and says, "I don't want any of my students to feel that way."
rationales.universalism: He brings in speakers from multiple cultural communities so students can see that "the world is bigger than their own street" and learn across differences.

rationales.tradition: He spends the day making dumplings for "Lunar New Year prep" and values the repeated family stories and inherited techniques.
rationales.benevolence: His gratitude that "she's still here to correct me" shows warm appreciation for time with his mother and family.
He frames his purpose around helping a specific child "feel whole from the start" and says that is "why I became a teacher."
rationales.universalism: He wants a Chinese-American student to feel pride rather than exclusion, reflecting concern for dignity and inclusion across identity lines.

rationales.tradition: He keeps revising a speech about the restaurant as "history" and practices it in Cantonese for the anniversary dinner.
rationales.benevolence: He is trying to honor what the restaurant means to "our family" and to the town where people celebrated and mourned together.

rationales.self_direction: He makes his own priority call, saying "Some things are more important than faculty meetings" and accepting the consequences.
rationales.conformity: He leaves the faculty meeting early despite school expectations and knows the principal noticed.
rationales.tradition: He insists on honoring the restaurant as "proof that we made it here" and refuses to let the meeting interfere with that family obligation.
rationales.benevolence: He acts because "my grandmother is counting on me" and "the whole family is counting on me."

rationales.self_direction: He resists family concern and says, "I'm not going to apologize" for pursuing the project he wants.
rationales.stimulation: He is drawn to the Chocó expedition as "exactly the kind of opportunity that doesn't come twice."
rationales.achievement: He emphasizes having built the company from "nothing" and wanting "something more than just coasting," showing strong drive for growth and success.
rationales.security: He dismisses the safer option of stability, rejecting "coasting" even while the company is already stretched thin.

rationales.stimulation: He loves "standing somewhere new, seeing what could be," which directly embraces novelty and exploration.

rationales.tradition: He cooks sancocho because "that's what Abuela always made in October" and treasures using her cracked wooden spoon.
rationales.benevolence: He keeps the noisy Sunday gathering because being with family "feels like it matters more than whatever else I could be doing."

rationales.self_direction: He notes that he used what his father taught him but "pointed it at something he never would have chosen," underscoring an independently chosen path.
rationales.achievement: He describes the proposal as "clean, professional, ambitious" and intentionally asks for more to build the project well.
rationales.security: He asks for "twice the funding we technically need because I want a buffer," explicitly prioritizing protection against risk.
rationales.tradition: He reflects on inheritance from his father and frames his current work as an expression of what he was taught at home.
rationales.universalism: He wants local guides from Chocó communities involved "from day one, not as an afterthought," showing respect for fair inclusion of affected communities.

rationales.stimulation: He says that when he is "navigating a new river" or negotiating in a new place, he feels most like who he is supposed to be.
rationales.security: He calls family life "the anchor" and says movement matters because you need "something to come back to."
rationales.tradition: He values the repeated family rhythm with his mother and Sunday meals, describing it as a longstanding pattern of how they relate.
rationales.benevolence: He personally drives to pick up his mother, tolerates her complaints, and still experiences the time together as "more than fine."

rationales.achievement: He is deeply moved by the project being approved with "full funding," reacting with tears and pride after a major professional win.

rationales.security: What hits him hardest is that a key employee left for something "more stable" right before the biggest project, highlighting strong concern about organizational steadiness and vulnerability.

rationales.stimulation: He admires María's "No drama, just forward motion" and says routine, same-menu work is not "my kind of honor," favoring movement over steadiness.
rationales.tradition: He marks Day of the Dead with the family ofrenda and tells Tomás "we do it anyway, because the doing is what matters."
rationales.benevolence: He invests in María by trusting her with a bigger role and shares family ritual with Tomás, showing care for close relationships.

rationales.stimulation: He is pulled toward the uncertain edge of the project, saying "The gap between dreaming and doing is where I live. Sometimes it feels like flying."
rationales.security: He openly inhabits uncertainty, worrying the reality may not match the vision and accepting that "I guess we'll find out."
rationales.universalism: His vision centers "community members who see themselves in the project, not just as employees" and guests leaving more changed and aware.

rationales.stimulation: He feels seen when his mother recognizes he needs "the adventure part" and that it is "how I'm built."

rationales.self_direction: He accepts "this is who I am" and says the guilt lifted when he stopped expecting everyone else to understand his path.
rationales.stimulation: Despite physical discomfort, he says "my mind is alive" after days of route-mapping, travel, and uncertainty in Chocó.
rationales.tradition: He explicitly reconciles movement with heritage, concluding you can "honor where you came from and still keep moving."
rationales.benevolence: He actively supports María's growth, telling her "she has a future here if she wants it," and celebrates her capability.
rationales.universalism: He stresses respect for local communities and environment, saying "We can't bring tourists in like we own the place" and must "earn the right to be there."
